title: Check if a Graph is Bipartite
description:
Given an undirected graph represented as adjacency lists, determine whether it is bipartite. Return True if possible to color nodes using two colors so that no edge connects nodes of the same color.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ V ≤ 10^5
- 0 ≤ E ≤ 2·10^5
explanation:
Intuition
BFS color nodes alternately. If we ever see an edge to an already colored neighbor with the same color, the graph is not bipartite.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Initialize color array with 0
2. Main Algorithm: For each uncolored node, BFS, assigning 1 or -1 alternately
3. Return Result: Check conflicts on edges

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(V+E)
- Space Complexity: O(V)

Key Insights
- Handle disconnected graphs by scanning all nodes
- Either BFS or DFS works

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting to start BFS from every component
- Using three colors incorrectly
hints: Conflict when neighbor has same color
